title: Component Definition v2
description: Contract for all components in the agentic design system. Every field that governs generation or evolution is machine-checkable. Prose fields exist for human and AI comprehension only; the validator never reads them.

name.description: Custom element name. Must contain a hyphen per the Custom Elements spec.

version.description: Semver. Fixed components may only change via human-authored major/minor bumps.

mutability.description: The stability class. fixed: structure, API, and styling are locked; regeneration requests are rejected at pipeline entry. adaptive: structure and API are locked (enforced by structural hash and API signature match); styling may vary only through context-tier token bindings. generative: structure, styling, and variants may evolve, but every mutation passes the full constraint pipeline and the evolution gate.

api.description: The component's public contract. For fixed and adaptive components, the canonical hash of this object must match the registry entry; any drift is a pipeline rejection.

enum.description: If present, the validator rejects generated usage with values outside this set.

allowedElements.description: Element or component names permitted in this slot. Empty array means text only. Absent means unrestricted.

tokens.description: Token consumption contract. Components may reference semantic-tier and context-tier tokens only. Any reference to a primitive-tier token, or to a token not listed in consumes, is a pipeline rejection.

status.description: Challengers exist only under an evolution gate. A challenger that fails its gate window is auto-deprecated. Two incumbents for the same context is a registry integrity error.

composition.description: Machine-checkable composition rules. Replaces v1 prose constraints.

constraints.description: Every constraint maps 1:1 to a validator module. No constraint types outside this enum exist; if a rule cannot be expressed here, it is not a constraint, it is a wish.

rule.description: Type-specific parameters consumed by the matching validator module. See constraint-enforcement-spec.md for each module's rule shape.

failureBehavior.description: reject: component never registers. flag: registers with a warning record and enters the human review queue. Default posture for the POC is reject; flag requires justification in rationale.

evolution.description: Required when mutability is generative or any variant has status challenger.

supersedes.description: name@version of the definition this one replaces.

threshold.description: Minimum relative improvement over incumbent required to ship. E.g. 0.05 means challenger must beat incumbent by 5%.

provenance.description: Who or what authored this definition, and why. Every AI-generated definition must carry a justification: the component explains its own choices. Registry entries without valid provenance are unreachable at generation time.

justification.description: Required when author is ai. The design rationale in plain language: what was decided, what was rejected, and why. This is the 'here is the call I made' record.
